title: Asia-Pacific Quality Assurance Framework
long_description: The APEC Quality Assurance Framework adds value to project development, approval and evaluation processes, provides a resource framework for APEC Working Groups and makes APEC projects more results and beneficiary orientated, to provide maximum value for money, to contribute to APEC's goals and enjoy wide participation from member economies. Funding under this initiative by the Australian Department of Infrastructure and Regional Development operates under the QAF and supports improvements in transport security in 5 APEC economies by targeting three distinct areas: transport security programs, regulatory and policy development, and quality assurance. The proposal will assist the APEC economies to identify transport security risks and to priorities.

title: Rehabilitation Of Railway In Cambodia: Support For Families Affected By Cambodian Railway
short_description: REHABILITATION OF RAILWAY IN CAMBODIA: SUPPORT FOR FAMILIES AFFECTED BY CAMBODIAN RAILWAY
long_description: The Rehabilitation of Railway in Cambodia Project supports the Asian Development Bank to upgrade the national railway of Cambodia: southern railway (254km from Sihanoukville to Phnom Penh) and northern railway (388 km from Phnom Penh to Poipet on Thai border). The majority of this funding will purchase rail sleepers, fund rehabilitation activities, and undertake related public works and upgrades at railway stations. The improved rail line will increase container traffic and trade between Cambodia and its neighbours, and reduce costs associated with road maintenance. The total value of this initiative is $27.1 million over a 7 year period, starting 2008-09.

title: Cao Lanh Bridge - Social Development
long_description: Funding under this initiative provides support to the Asia Development Bank for the Cao Lanh Bridge initiative. The project includes complement social development work such as improving capacity of local health services to cope with increased demand, restoring livelihoods, road safety awareness. The total value of this initiative is $130.2 million over 6 years, starting 2011-12.

title: Roads For Development: Monitoring And Phase 2 Design
short_description: ROADS FOR DEVELOPMENT: MONITORING AND PHASE 2 DESIGN
long_description: The Roads for Development program scales up the effective work undertaken by the Investment Budget Execution Support for Rural Infrastructure Development and Employment Generation (TIM Works) project. Its objective is to establish and institutionalise a rural road system for effectively planning, budgeting and managing rural road works using labour based methods, as appropriate. The program will enable more rural women and men to benefit from improved access to roads and therefore access to markets. Improved roads will help farmers to earn higher and more sustained income and will make it easier for people to access health centres, schools and markets. This program will rehabilitate and maintain rural roads and will ensure that at least 1,150km, more than a third of the rural road network in Timor-Leste is kept in good condition. Where possible labour intensive approaches will be used that will maximise the employment opportunities for people in rural areas. This could result in short-term employment for over 50,000 people, providing much needed work experience and extra cash to village communities. The total value of this initiative is $30.5 million over five years, starting 2011-12.

title: Eastern Indonesia National Roads Improvement Project Management Support And Supervision
short_description: EASTERN INDONESIA NATIONAL ROADS IMPROVEMENT PROJECT MANAGEMENT SUPPORT AND SUPERVISION
long_description: Funding for this initiative is the grant component for support to the Eastern Indonesia National Roads Improvement Project (EINRIP) Loan. The period of this grant will extend to three years after loan completion to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the EINRIP impact. This initiative is expected to cover EINRIP-related costs such as: project preparation (development of the initial activity work plans and initial feasibility, economic, social, environmental and technical assessments), technical assistance and training on issues such as road safety management, engineering design, road maintenance, financial management and anti-corruption work, monitoring and evaluation of EINRIP road's performance and impact, technical and Financial Audits of EINRIP project implementation, budgeting and policy reform to assist to mitigate the problems created by overloaded vehicles, and monitoring and evaluation studies. The total value of this initiative is $36.5 million over 11 years, starting 2005-06.

title: Cambodian Rehabilitation Of Railway: Administered Infrastructure Support
short_description: CAMBODIAN REHABILITATION OF RAILWAY: ADMINISTERED INFRASTRUCTURE SUPPORT
long_description: The Rehabilitation of Railway in Cambodia Project supports the Asian Development Bank to upgrade the national railway of Cambodia: southern railway (254km from Sihanoukville to Phnom Penh) and northern railway (388 km from Phnom Penh to Poipet on Thai border). The majority of this funding will purchase rail sleepers, fund rehabilitation activities, and undertake related public works and upgrades at railway stations. The improved rail line will increase container traffic and trade between Cambodia and its neighbours, and reduce costs associated with road maintenance. The total value of this initiative is $27.1 million over a 7 year period, starting 2008-09.

title: Papua New Guinea-Australia Transport Sector Support Program
short_description: PAPUA NEW GUINEA-AUSTRALIA TRANSPORT SECTOR SUPPORT PROGRAM
long_description: The Papua New Guinea-Australia Transport Sector Support Program Phase II will assist PNG to achieve 75% of priority roads in 'good' condition and improved compliance with international safety and security standards particularly for aviation and maritime sectors. The total value of this initiative is $400.0 million over 6 years, with some components starting in 2013-14.

title: Supporting Business Associations In Vietnam
long_description: The Supporting Business Associations in Vietnam project will strengthen the capacity of Vietnamese business and cooperative associations to work effectively with their members to improve competitiveness and income generation through two components: Component 1: enhance capacity of business and cooperative associations to support their members and contribute to evidence-based policy making. Activities will support business associations to improve their own operations, encouraging business associations to work with their members on improving sector standards and participate in policy dialogue with the government, particularly around trade issues, and Component 2: improve the ability of cooperative associations to engage in policy advocacy and support them to improve their income generation. Activities will assist cooperatives to meet the new standards set out in the revised Cooperative Law and advocate for appropriate policies in the implementation of the revised Cooperative Law. This component will also support work with select cooperatives to increase their competitiveness in the global marketplace and provide increased incomes to their members. The total value of this initiative is $1.6 million over 3 years, starting 2013-14.

title: Maritime And Aviation Security Awareness Workshops
short_description: MARITIME AND AVIATION SECURITY AWARENESS WORKSHOPS
long_description: Funding under this initiative supports security awareness in the aviation and maritime sectors across the Asia Pacific region and will bring together 13 of the 21 APEC economies: Indonesia, the Philippines, Thailand, Viet Nam, Malaysia, China, Timor-Leste, the Republic of Korea, Papua New Guinea, Hong Kong, Brunei, the United States, Singapore and Australia.

title: Solomon Islands Transport: National Transport Fund
short_description: SOLOMON ISLANDS TRANSPORT: NATIONAL TRANSPORT FUND
long_description: The Solomon Islands Transport Sector Based Approach initiative will support the Solomon Islands Government implement a series of activities in the Transport sector including the Transport Sector Development Project, Malaita Road works, Technical Assistance support to the Solomon Islands Government Ministry of Infrastructure Development and design and Fiduciary risk assessment activities. Majority of funding to the Sector will be channelled through the National Transport Fund to support the priorities under the National Transport Plan. The total value of this initiative is $37.554 million over 5 years, starting 2010-11.

title: Vanuatu Transport Sector Support Program: Direct Funding
short_description: VANUATU TRANSPORT SECTOR SUPPORT PROGRAM: DIRECT FUNDING
long_description: The Vanuatu Transport Sector Support Program (VTSSP) will be a long term, 10 to 15 year sector based program delivered through a number of phases. The first phase was a 5 year program working with the Vanuatu Public Works Department to complete three sub-projects and related works on the islands of Tanna, Malekula and Ambae, and other works on these three islands. Phase 1 of VTSSP rehabilitated 10% of the rural road network, generating employment including for women. Funding of this initiative is for VTSSP Phase 2 which aims to: ensure the Ministry of Infrastructure and Public Utilities (MIPU) and the Public Works Department (PWD) has the skills, systems and resources necessary to plan and manage its national road management mandate,  and that the PWD effectively maintains key road transport infrastructure in three provinces - Penama, Malampa, and Tafea. The total value of this initiative is $27.3 million over 4 years, starting 2012-13.

title: Preventative Aviation Security Enhancement
long_description: Funding under this initiative provided by the Australian Department of Infrastructure and Regional Development aims to assist Indonesia to build capacity in transport security regulation, through: incident prevention management framework and exercises to build the structures needed to record and manage aviation security incidents across the country and utilise data, analyse results and make recommendations, develop a new national background checking policy, along with the relevant regulatory material, and expand the capabilities of the DGCA Aviation Security team to include training facilitation skills and training program management.

title: Provincial Road Improvement And Maintenance: Pilot Program
short_description: PROVINCIAL ROAD IMPROVEMENT AND MAINTENANCE: PILOT PROGRAM
long_description: The Provincial Road Improvement and Maintenance (PRIM) Pilot Program is an output-based grant that incentivise the local government to implement road maintenance and rehabilitation works. It aims at improving the way local government plans, budgets and implements the investment in road maintenance on a sustainable basis. It will implement best practice maintenance and rehabilitation, strengthen existing government systems, raise accountability and transparency, implement effective monitoring and evaluation and train provincial staff, consultants and contractors. The Government of Indonesia seeks to broaden the use of the hibah (on-granting) mechanism that was successfully piloted for water and sanitation sector to the roads sector. The total value of this initiative is $11.9 million over 2 years, starting 213-14.

title: Provincial Road Management Facility:  Rehabilitation And Maintainance Of 1000Km Of Provincial Roads
short_description: PROVINCIAL ROAD MANAGEMENT FACILITY:  REHABILITATION AND MAINTAINANCE OF 1000KM OF PROVINCIAL ROADS
long_description: The Provincial Road Management Facility (PRMF) assists the Philippine Government rehabilitate and maintain more than 1000km of provincial roads in up to 10 provinces in Mindanao and the Visayas. PRMF aims to boost the local economy by improving road transport so that more people will have better access to rehabilitated and maintained road networks, economic activity and public infrastructure and services that will improve their livelihoods. Initial reports document improvements for communities in: travel times (a reduction by as much as 50 per cent in some areas), employment opportunities for local unskilled workers, economic activity for businesses along roads, increased investment by farmers in agricultural production.  The total value of this initiative is $100.0 million over 6 years, starting 2009-10.

title: Eastern Indonesia National Road Improvement Program: Implementation
short_description: EASTERN INDONESIA NATIONAL ROAD IMPROVEMENT PROGRAM: IMPLEMENTATION
long_description: The Eastern Indonesia National Road Improvement Program (EINRIP) provides a highly concessional loan and grant funded assistance for major reconstruction and improvement of national roads and bridges in Eastern Indonesia to promote the economic and social development of the region. EINRIP is supporting 20 major road projects in nine provinces, totalling around 395km of roads and 1,300 m associated fabricated steel bridge structures. Assistance also includes an EINRIP Monitoring Unit to provide day-to-day technical oversight of Government's management of the program, a Project Preparation Consultant to provide Australian and Indonesian technical expertise for planning and technical design for all 20 road projects, a program of Technical and Financial Audits to closely assess construction quality and advise on remedial measures necessary to ensure compliance with design specifications, and a Monitoring and Evaluation program that extends beyond the life of the project to assess its long-term impact. The program was a flagship component of the Australia Indonesia Partnership for Reconstruction and Development resulting from the $1billion assistance package announced after the 2004 tsunami. As a loan project, implementation is handled through Government of Indonesia systems with the Directorate General of Highways at the Ministry of Public Works fully responsible for the management of all civil works contractors and engineering supervision consultants. The total value of Australia's assistance under this initiative is $285.0 million over 9 years, starting 2007-08.

title: Transport Sector Support Program Implementation Service Provider
short_description: TRANSPORT SECTOR SUPPORT PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION SERVICE PROVIDER
long_description: The Papua New Guinea-Australia Transport Sector Support Program Phase II will assist PNG to achieve 75% of priority roads in 'good' condition by 2015 and improved compliance with international safety and security standards particularly for aviation and maritime sectors. The total value of this initiative is $500.0 million over 5 years, starting 2013-14.

title: Transport Sector Support Program Phase 2:  Improved Compliance With Safety And Security Standards
short_description: TRANSPORT SECTOR SUPPORT PROGRAM PHASE 2:  IMPROVED COMPLIANCE WITH SAFETY AND SECURITY STANDARDS
long_description: The Papua New Guinea-Australia Transport Sector Support Program Phase 2 will assist PNG to achieve 75% of priority roads in 'good' condition and improved compliance with international safety and security standards particularly for aviation and maritime sectors. The total value of this initiative is $400.0 million over 6 years, with some components starting in 2013-14.

title: Kiribati Road Rehabilitation
long_description: Poor infrastructure is a key inhibitor to economic growth in Kiribati. In recognising the importance of improved economic infrastructure provision, AusAID and the Government of Kiribati (GoK) agreed, during partnership talks held in September 2012, to add infrastructure as the fourth priority under the Kiribati-Australia Partnership for Development. This initiative provides support for economic infrastructure in Kiribati in cooperation with other development partners through the Pacific Region Infrastructure Facility (PRIF). Through PRIF, AusAID funds other development partners – such as the World Bank and Asian Development Bank (ADB) – to deliver infrastructure projects in Kiribati. Support is provided in sub sectors of: transport, road rehabilitation, water sanitation, energy and ICT. The total value of this initiative is $34.4 million over five years, starting 2011-12.

title: Indonesia Transport Safety Assistance Package
long_description: The Indonesia Transport Safety Assistance Package (ITSAP) is designed to contribute to improved transport safety in Indonesia. ITSAP will assist Indonesia to regulate and promote transport safety in accordance with applicable international standards and contemporary safety management practices, consistent with the priorities of the Indonesian Government, by providing technical assistance, capacity building, and advise on improved governance and safety management practices and procedures. The first phase of ITSAP began in 2007 following the Garuda air crash in Yogyakarta. Since 2007, the Department of Infrastructure and Regional Development has administered the $38.4 million ITSAP program, which is funded by Australia's Official Development Assistance. Assistance is delivered by Australian agencies including Air services Australia, the Australian Maritime Safety Authority, the Australian Transport Safety Bureau and the Civil Aviation Safety Authority to their Indonesian counterparts including the Directorate General of Civil Aviation, the Directorate General of Sea Transportation and the Directorate General of Land Transportation within the Ministry of Transportation.  Australian agencies also work with the National Transportation Safety Committee, the National Search and Rescue Agency and AirNav Indonesia. The total value of this initiative is $4.6 million expensed during the 2014-15 financial year.

title: Roads For Development: Timor Leste
long_description: The Roads for Development program scales up the effective work undertaken by the Investment Budget Execution Support for Rural Infrastructure Development and Employment Generation (TIM Works) project. Its objective is to establish and institutionalise a rural road system for effectively planning, budgeting and managing rural road works using labour based methods, as appropriate. The program will enable more rural women and men to benefit from improved access to roads and therefore access to markets. Improved roads will help farmers to earn higher and more sustained income and will make it easier for people to access health centres, schools and markets. This program will rehabilitate and maintain rural roads and will ensure that at least 1,150km, more than a third of the rural road network in Timor-Leste is kept in good condition. Where possible labour intensive approaches will be used that will maximise the employment opportunities for people in rural areas. This could result in short-term employment for over 50,000 people, providing much needed work experience and extra cash to village communities. The total value of this initiative is $30.5 million over five years, starting 2011-12.

title: Cao Lanh Civil Works In Vietnam
short_description: CAO LANH CIVIL WORKS IN VIETNAM
long_description: Funding under this initiative provides support to the Asia Development Bank for the Cao Lanh Bridge initiative. The project includes complement social development work such as improving capacity of local health services to cope with increased demand, restoring livelihoods, road safety awareness. The total value of this initiative is $130.2 million over 6 years, starting 2011-12.

title: Public-Private Partnerships In Transport Infrastructure Development
short_description: PUBLIC-PRIVATE PARTNERSHIPS IN TRANSPORT INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT
long_description: The Department of Infrastructure will facilitate the development of a framework specifically tailored to assist Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, Philippines, Thailand and Viet Nam to prioritize, develop and implement Public-Private Partnerships to facilitate transport supply chain connectivity.

title: Reducing Post-Harvest Losses by Improving Transport Supply Chains
short_description: REDUCING POST-HARVEST LOSSES BY IMPROVING TRANSPORT SUPPLY CHAINS
long_description: Reducing post-harvest losses by improving transport supply chains will focus on improving the transport component of supply chains, delivering practical measures and solutions to reduce post-harvest losses to maximize fresh produce to consumers in Indonesia, Papua New Guinean, Philippines, Thailand and Viet Nam.

title: Cambodia Road Asset Maintenance Project
long_description: The Cambodia Road Asset Maintenance Project supports the World Bank's and Asian Development Bank's Road Asset Maintenance Project in Cambodia which aims to improve the maintenance of priority Cambodian roads and to build the capacity of the Ministry of Public Works & Transport-Directorate General of Public Works to plan and maintain the road network. The total value of this initiative is $6.0 million over 4 years, starting 2007-08.

title: Australia-Indonesia Maritime And Aviation Transport Security Project
short_description: AUSTRALIA-INDONESIA MARITIME AND AVIATION TRANSPORT SECURITY PROJECT
long_description: Funding under this initiative supports professional short-term (five and a half week) attachments for 16 Indonesian Ministry of Transport officials, eight from within Directorate General of Civil Aviation and eight from the Directorate General of Sea Transportation to strengthen their transport security skills and expertise. The officials will undertake on-the-job training and short-term training courses in security audit activities and standards.

title: Infrastructure Program Implementation
long_description: The Samoa Economic Infrastructure Program aims to stimulate sustainable economic activity in Samoa and provide improved access to services and markets by investing in economic infrastructure. The program comprises three components: investments in priority economic infrastructure projects, infrastructure structural and policy reforms performance linked aid program, and a technical assistance fund. The program uses established and experienced infrastructure partners such as the World Bank and the ADB to implement specific infrastructure investments. The total value of this initiative is $20.0 million over 4 years, starting 2013/2014.

title: Cao Lanh Detailed Design And Construction Supervision
short_description: CAO LANH DETAILED DESIGN AND CONSTRUCTION SUPERVISION
long_description: Australia is working with the Governments of Vietnam and the Republic of Korea through the Asian Development Bank on the Central Mekong Delta Connectivity Project (CMDCP). The funding for this initiatives forms part of Australia's commitment to CMDCP, specifically the detailed designing and supervision of Cao Lanh Bridge and its approach roads of Central Mekong Delta Region Connectivity. The total value of this initiative is $27.1 million over seven years, starting 2010-11.

title: Growth Initiative: Program Evaluation And Monitoring
short_description: GROWTH INITIATIVE: PROGRAM EVALUATION AND MONITORING
long_description: The Australian Government launched the Infrastructure for Growth Initiative (IFGI) in 2007 to help regional partners address their pressing infrastructure needs. IFGI is directed towards helping regional partner government improve their infrastructure policies and finance high-priority infrastructure in conjunction with other international donors. The Indonesia Infrastructure Initiative (IndII) has been developed within the IFGI framework and is supported by funding under IFGI. and aims to support national and sub-national governments' efforts to address constraints on infrastructure investment and enable efficient and effective infrastructure service delivery. The total value of this initiative is $178.6 million over 8 years, starting 2007-08.

title: Vanuatu Transport Sector Support Program: Administered Program Support and Delivery
short_description: VANUATU TRANSPORT SECTOR SUPPORT PROGRAM: ADMINISTERED PROGRAM SUPPORT AND DELIVERY
long_description: The Vanuatu Transport Sector Support Program (VTSSP) will be a long term, 10 to 15 year sector based program delivered through a number of phases. The first phase was a 5 year program working with the Vanuatu Public Works Department to complete three sub-projects and related works on the islands of Tanna, Malekula and Ambae, and other works on these three islands. Phase 1 of VTSSP rehabilitated 10% of the rural road network, generating employment including for women. Funding of this initiative is for VTSSP Phase 2 which aims to: ensure the Ministry of Infrastructure and Public Utilities (MIPU) and the Public Works Department (PWD) has the skills, systems and resources necessary to plan and manage its national road management mandate,  and that the PWD effectively maintains key road transport infrastructure in three provinces - Penama, Malampa, and Tafea. The total value of this initiative is $27.3 million over 4 years, starting 2012-13.

title: Vanuatu Transport Sector Support Program: Program Management Support and Delivery
short_description: VANUATU TRANSPORT SECTOR SUPPORT PROGRAM: PROGRAM MANAGEMENT SUPPORT AND DELIVERY
long_description: The Vanuatu Transport Sector Support Program (VTSSP) will be a long term, 10 to 15 year sector based program delivered through a number of phases. The first phase was a 5 year program working with the Vanuatu Public Works Department to complete three sub-projects and related works on the islands of Tanna, Malekula and Ambae, and other works on these three islands. Phase 1 of VTSSP rehabilitated 10% of the rural road network, generating employment including for women. Funding of this initiative is for VTSSP Phase 2 which aims to: ensure the Ministry of Infrastructure and Public Utilities (MIPU) and the Public Works Department (PWD) has the skills, systems and resources necessary to plan and manage its national road management mandate,  and that the PWD effectively maintains key road transport infrastructure in three provinces - Penama, Malampa, and Tafea. The total value of this initiative is $27.3 million over 4 years, starting 2012-13.

title: Provincial Road Management Facility: Monitoring and Evaluation
short_description: PROVINCIAL ROAD MANAGEMENT FACILITY: MONITORING AND EVALUATION
long_description: The Provincial Road Management Facility (PRMF) assists the Philippine Government rehabilitate and maintain more than 1000km of provincial roads in up to 10 provinces in Mindanao and the Visayas. PRMF aims to boost the local economy by improving road transport so that more people will have better access to rehabilitated and maintained road networks, economic activity and public infrastructure and services that will improve their livelihoods. Initial reports document improvements for communities in: travel times (a reduction by as much as 50 per cent in some areas), employment opportunities for local unskilled workers, economic activity for businesses along roads, increased investment by farmers in agricultural production.  The total value of this initiative is $100.0 million over 6 years, starting 2009-10.

title: Improving International Aviation Security  to Enhance the Levels of Training for Aviation Security Officials in the Philippines and Pacific Countries
short_description: IMPROVING INTERNATIONAL AVIATION SECURITY  TO ENHANCE THE LEVELS OF TRAINING FOR AVIATION SECURITY OFFICIALS IN THE PHILIPPINES AND PACIFIC COUNTRIES
long_description: Funding under this initiative by the Office of Transport Security (OTS) aims to enhance the levels of training for aviation security officials in the Philippines and Pacific Island Countries. An aviation security training centre was established in the Philippines. Activities under this initiative will support the centre and attendance at International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) accredited security courses.

title: Cao Lanh Bridge - Monitoring and Advisory Service
short_description: CAO LANH BRIDGE - MONITORING AND ADVISORY SERVICE
long_description: Funding under this initiative provides support to the Asia Development Bank for the Cao Lanh Bridge initiative. The project includes complement social development work such as improving capacity of local health services to cope with increased demand, restoring livelihoods, road safety awareness. title: Last Port Of Call Capacity: Building Aviation Security Capacity
short_description: LAST PORT OF CALL CAPACITY: BUILDING AVIATION SECURITY CAPACITY
long_description: Funding under this initiative by the Office of Transport Security (OTS) aims to target aviation security capacity building activities of 'Last Port Of Call' countries enroute to Australia. Assistance will be provided in the form of training, mentoring and information sharing on CTCF projects, maritime safety and security standards.

title: Papua New Guinea-Australia Transport Sector Support Program: Advisory Support
short_description: PAPUA NEW GUINEA-AUSTRALIA TRANSPORT SECTOR SUPPORT PROGRAM: ADVISORY SUPPORT
long_description: The Papua New Guinea-Australia Transport Sector Support Program Phase II will assist PNG to achieve 75% of priority roads in 'good' condition and improved compliance with international safety and security standards particularly for aviation and maritime sectors. The total value of this initiative is $400.0 million over 6 years, with some components starting in 2013-14.

short_description: SAMOA ECONOMIC INFRASTRUCTURE PROGRAM: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE
long_description: The Samoa Economic Infrastructure Program aims to stimulate sustainable economic activity in Samoa and provide improved access to services and markets by investing in economic infrastructure. The program comprises three components: investments in priority economic infrastructure projects, infrastructure structural and policy reforms performance linked aid program, and a technical assistance fund. The program uses established and experienced infrastructure partners such as the World Bank and the ADB to implement specific infrastructure investments. The total value of this initiative is $20.0 million over 4 years, starting 2013/2014.

title: Solomon Islands Transport Sector Based Approach: Ministry Of Infrastructure Development Technical Assistance
short_description: SOLOMON ISLANDS TRANSPORT SECTOR BASED APPROACH: MINISTRY OF INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE
long_description: The Solomon Islands Transport Sector Based Approach initiative will support the Solomon Islands Government implement a series of activities in the Transport sector including the Transport Sector Development Project, Malaita Road works, Technical Assistance support to the Solomon Islands Government Ministry of Infrastructure Development and design and Fiduciary risk assessment activities. Majority of funding to the Sector will be channelled through the National Transport Fund to support the priorities under the National Transport Plan. The total value of this initiative is $37.554 million over 5 years, starting 2010-11.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Private Infrastructure Finance (IL&FS) Project
long_description: The objectives of the Private Infrastructure Finance Project are to: 1) develop prototype contractual arrangements for private investment in the Infrastructure Leasing and Financial Services Limited's (IL&FS) areas of involvement, thereby facilitating entry of the private sector on a much larger scale in areas heretore dominated by the public sector, 2) build up India's capacity to attract private investment in infrastructure, pilot-test institutional and contractual arrangements in a variety of subprojects under various administrative and political conditions, and help establish a track record as a prerequisite for large scale private investment in the sector, 3) meet the pressing needs of commercial infrastructure project entities for long-term rupee financing, 4) foster efficiency in the delivery and use of selected infrastructure services, 5) encourage the establishment of more efficient practices in the construction and operation of infrastructure project, and 6) assist in alleviating the severe financial and institutional constraints to the expansion of infrastructure in India. The project consists of three interrelated components: 1) an investment component in the form of a line of credit to IL&FS, 2) a subproject preparation component, which will cover specialized consultancy services, and 3) a training and technical assistance component, which will include: (a) IL&FS's staff development for its staff, and (b) specific studies that IL&FS is undertaking to facilitate the evaluation and implementation of commercial infrastructure projects.

title: Andhra Pradesh State Highway Project
long_description: The development objectives of the Andhra Pradesh State Highway Project are to: 1) reduce transport costs and transport constraints on economic activity, and 2) improve the state's institutional capabilities to manage road programs and assets.  The project consists of the following components: (i) widening and strengthening of priority roads, including civil works, design and supervision and land acquisition, resettlement and rehabilitation, (ii) maintenance of state roads, comprising works and technical assistance for maintenance management, (iii) reform and strengthening of the State road agency, including technical assistance, training and equipment, and (iv) repayment of Project Preparation Facility.

title: Andhra Pradesh Hazard Mitigation and Emergency Cyclone Recovery Project
short_description: ANDHRA PRADESH HAZARD MITIGATION AND EMERGENCY CYCLONE RECOVERY PROJECT
long_description: The Andhra Pradesh Hazard Mitigation and Emergency Cyclone Recovery Project's objectives are (1) to help the state government prepare and implement a hazard management program in high risk areas, and restore public infrastructure lost last year, according to the hazard-resistant criteria for infrastructure sites, construction, and quality control, and (2) to enhance India's cyclone early warning capacity. The project will finance (1) a hazard management program promoting selected prevention, mitigation, and preparedness activities for water-related disasters, (2) the restoration of damaged public infrastructure, including irrigation drains and flood-banks, roads and bridges, public buildings, and mitigation works, and (3) technical assistance for project implementation and management, and financing for incremental operating costs on a declining scale.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Tamil Nadu Road Sector Project
long_description: The Tamil Nadu Road Sector Project seeks to improve the quality, and sustainability of the state's core road network, which refers to the state highways and major district roads being managed by the Highways Department (HD) of Tamil Nadu. The three project components will address: 1) road upgrading, aimed at improving the capacity of the state road network, and includes as well construction of bridges, and state roads. Appropriate environmental mitigation, and resettlement plans will be developed, 2) road maintenance, and safety works component, through periodic road maintenance, and safety related works, implemented through a mix of conventional maintenance, and performance-based maintenance contracts, supervised with periodic technical reviews by an independent consultant, and, 3) institutional strengthening, and policy development, utilizing the services of experts, and consultants, to support the development, and use of a maintenance management system. Capacity building of agencies concerned in road safety will be supported through technical assistance, and training services.

title: Andhra Pradesh Economic Restructuring Project
long_description: The Andhra Pradesh Economic Restructuring Project seeks to provide resources to meet priority needs in human development (primary health, nutrition, and primary education) and in the maintenance of economic assets affecting the rural poor (irrigation, rural roads, and core access roads). There are six project components. First, the district primary education component will finance construction of new schools, recruitment of additional teachers, and establishment of school improvement funds for 14 educationally disadvantaged districts in The Andhra Pradesh. Second, the primary health component will improve the quality of primary health care services, support capacity building, and enhance community participation. Third, the nutrition component will improve nutrition and health of poor pre-school-aged children and women by providing training and increasing the quality of the Integrated Child Development Service program. Fourth, the rural road upgrading and maintenance component will provide basic all-weather road access to villages. Fifth, the irrigation rehabilitation and maintenance component will improve the State's irrigation through farmers' participation in irrigation management and improved cost recovery. Sixth, the public enterprise reform component will assist the Government in meeting the cost of severance pay as part of the on-going reform of public enterprises.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: KARNATAKA STATE HIGHWAYS IMPROVEMENT PROJECT

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development

title: Grand Trunk Road Improvement Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Mumbai Urban Transport Project
long_description: The Mumbai Urban Transport Project was approved by the Bank's Board of Directors on June 18, 2002 and its restructuring approve on September 30, 2008 by the Board. Every component is progressing satisfactorily except for the road construction component that has experienced long delays and still suffers from uncertainty in interagency coordination and a few remaining challenges in resettlement and rehabilitation. The project includes an extremely large resettlement component (18,000 households and shops) that suffered difficulties resulting in an Inspection Panel investigation to which the Bank's management presented a remedial action plan that was discussed by the Board on March 28, 2006. Since then the quality of resettlement and rehabilitation under the project has regularly improved to become satisfactory and the resettlement is almost complete (96 percent complete as February 1st, 2010) while post-resettlement activities are exemplary. Out of the two major link roads that are included in the project, the widening of Jogeswari-Vikhroli-Link-Road is almost completed, but the creation of Santacruz-Chembur-Link-Road is still encountering important difficulties that leave uncertainty as to its successful completion in a reasonable time. The disbursement of the project amounts to 70 percent of the loan/credit amount on May 31, 2010. Overall the project is rated satisfactory for compliance with safeguards policies.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Highways Rehabilitation
long_description: The proposed additional loan for the Highways Rehabilitation Project for Pakistan will help finance the costs associated with restructuring of HRP to urgently address reconstruction/rehabilitation of four main highways that were damaged by the earthquake of October 8, 2005. There will be no change to the ongoing project activities. Most of the remaining funds have been committed, with a large number of contracts ongoing, and it will be important to complete ongoing activities as planned in order to achieve the original project development objective. The new component, to be implemented in parallel to the original components, will also be implemented by the National Highway Authority (NHA) through its fully staffed Project Management Unit (PMU) that has sufficient technical and fiduciary capacity.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Diversified Agricultural Support Project (DASP)
long_description: The principal objectives of the Uttar Pradesh Diversified Agricultural Support Project are to increase agricultural productivity, to promote private sector development, and to improve rural infrastructure. There are six project components. The first, support for technology development, enhances capacity for research coordination, establishes a competitive agricultural research program (CARP) to support time bound adaptive research on priority constraints, increases productivity of the smallholder agriculture, and strengthens research support for technology dissemination activities. The second establishes a demand-driven technology dissemination system through rationalizing, reorienting, and strengthening line departments, increases participation by the private sector in input supply and support services, increases participation by the farming communities mobilized with the help of nongovernmental organizations, and supports human resource development and greater use of information technology. The third supports increased private sector involvement and public/private partnerships in agri-business development. The fourth supports rural infrastructure development by improving rural roads in the project districts, rural markets, and market information collection and dissemination systems. The fifth supports a project management and enhanced capacity for economic policy analysis while the sixth supports a project preparation facility.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: INTEGRATED WATERSHED DEVELOPMENT PROJECT
long_description: The Integrated Watershed Development (Hills II) Project aims to improve India's productive potential in five states, using evolving watershed treatment technologies and community participatory approaches. The project contributes significantly to decreasing soil erosion, increasing water availability, and alleviating poverty in the contiguous areas of the Shivalik hills in the five project states. The project has two main components: 1) Watershed protection and development, and 2) Institutional strengthening. The first component includes watershed treatments, fodder and livestock development, and rural infrastructure development. The second component includes policy reforms, studies, and human resource development, beneficiary capacity building, income generating activities for women, information management and monitoring and evaluation, and support to project coordination.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Inland Waterways Project
long_description: The Inland Waterways Project will: (a) improve Inland Waterways (IWW) management and introduce organizational and financial reforms in line with a market economy, (b) expand IWW capacity by widening and deepening channels to improve navigation, which requires constructing dams to increase the water level, (c) improve connections between IWW and land transport services by appropriately equipping IWW ports, (d) generate hydroelectric power by utilizing the indifferential heads at the dams, and (e) enhance the capabilities of the IWW entities. Two of the project provinces - Hunan and Guangxi - are relatively poor, and project investments are expected to promote economic development there by helping to create new jobs, increase incomes and reduce poverty. The project's multisectoral strategy is consistent with the financial goals of the Government's IWW reforms policies, and addresses the lack of intersectoral coordination that reduced the IWW network in the past.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Tamil Nadu Urban Development Project (02)
long_description: The Second Tamil Nadu Urban Development Project will improve urban infrastructure services in a sustainable manner, and will build the institutional and financial capacities of the urban local bodies (ULBs). The project components include: 1) institutional development, to enhance the capacity building of ULBs and the Department of Municipal Administration and Water Supply (DMAWS). This component also include the provision of technical assistance to the Tamil Nadu Urban Development Fund (TNUDF) and ULBs regarding bond issuance, and to ULBs for project preparation and implementation, 2) urban investments, which will provide a line of credit through the TNUDF, as well as funds for an integrated sanitation program for the targeted urban poor. It is expected that the project will bring economic, health, and environmental benefits to the Tamil Nadu's urban population, including to its low-income segment population, through improvements in the quality, quantity, and sustainability of urban services.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: THIRD NATIONAL HIGHWAYS PROJECT
long_description: The Third National Highways Project for India aims to reduce transport constraints on economic activity and reduce transport costs, and to improve institutional capabilities to manage road programs, assets, and service. The project has three main components. The first component, highway upgrading, finances civil works for widening and strengthening the national highway from two lanes to a four-six lane divided carriageway with service roads. This component also supports environmental management including tree planting, project supervision, land acquisition, resettlement and utility relocation, and project management costs. The second component supports a program of maintenance works to introduce alternative contracting methods and to strengthen maintenance management practices. It also finances a program of minor improvements specifically targeted at supporting improved methods for traffic delays and accidents. The third component funds consultancy services to develop and implement an institutional strengthening program, for a road information system, for two pilot corridor management units, and to prepare minor improvement proposals as well as funding a road safety policy study and training and staff development programs.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Allahabad Bypass Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Rural Roads Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: GUJARAT STATE HIGHWAY PROJECT
long_description: The Gujarat State Highway Project, will build the capacity of the Government of Gujarat towards effective planning, and management of road infrastructure, and, in addition, the existing road infrastructure asset utilization will be maximized, through priority investments, and increased maintenance funding. The components call for: 1) widening, and strengthening of 800-900 km of high priority state highways, through civil works to be procured in three phases, 2) land acquisition, whose costs, and compensation will be borne by the state government. Conversely, the difference between compensation paid under the land acquisition act, and replacement value of assets acquired (paid in the form of assistance), infrastructure facilities, and resettlement/rehabilitation entitlements, such as allowances, training, and monitoring expenses, will be funded by the Bank, 3) reducing the periodic maintenance backlog of the state, by funding rehabilitation works, to be implemented over a three year period, 4) the design and supervision of civil works, to be implemented by international consultants, who will have full engineering responsibility, in addition to the preparation of bidding documents, 5) building the institutional capacity of the Roads and Buildings Department, as the manager of the state's road network. Technical assistance, training, and equipment supply will be provided, and, 6) funding techno-economic feasibility, and detailed engineering studies, required for a possible follow-up project.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Railway Project (07)
long_description: The Seventh Railway Project will support the Ministry of Railways' (MOR) initial efforts to implement its 30-point action statement of railway reform. The project's main objectives are to assist MOR: 1) redefine the relationship between the railway and the Government, 2) reshape the railway's organization and strengthen its internal management, 3) expand the railway's transport capacity, raise its investment efficiency, and enhance its finances, and 4) modernize the railway's technologies and bolster the efficiency and quality of its transport operations and service. To achieve these objectives, MOR formulated a series of components on policy reform and institutional development (PID), investments, and studies. PID components include: 1) railway enterprise reform, 2) railway tariff reform, and 3) labor productivity enhancement. Investments include: 1) expanding corridor capacity, 2) modernizing information systems, 3) upgrading telecommunications systems, 4) commercializing container transport, 5) enhancing environmental protection, and 6) expanding system capacity. The studies include components that will provide MOR with the technical assistance and software needed to prepare future railway projects or to develop and apply new railway technologies and management techniques.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Third Henan Provincial Highway Project
long_description: The Third Henan Provincial Highway Project aims at providing a more efficient and effective transport infrastructure in support of social and economic development in Henan Province. To meet this development objective, the project will produce the following outputs: reduced traffic and enhanced mobility, improved access to the poor areas in the province, and increased efficiency and effectiveness in public sector management of the road network while preparing the sector for commercialization opportunities. The project has three components. The first increases capacity by constructing an expressway, linking roads to maximize the impact of the expressway, and providing buildings/services areas, traffic engineering and equipment for operations and maintenance. The second component improves or rehabilitates roads with priority given to rural road segments that explicitly enable local people to have an easier access to education, health, and other social activities and facilitates production and business operations and benefits the local environment. The third component includes three sub-components which 1) build the capacity of the communications department, and provide extensive training programs in the areas of environmental protection, construction and maintenance technology and management, highway network planning, and operations management for senior professionals, 2) supply technical assistance for studies in road management improvement, and 3) provide specialized equipment

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Fujian Highway Sector Investment

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Karachi Port Improvement Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Shijiazhuang Urban Transport Project
long_description: The Shijiazhuang Urban Transport Project will foster the development of an efficient, and environmentally sustainable urban transport system in the city, and provide as well a wider set of commuting choices to its users. The components are: 1) strengthening the primary, and secondary road networks within the second ring, including construction of interchanges, and railway overpasses/underpasses, and, completion, and widening of existing roads, 2) provision of additional facilities for bicycle users, and pedestrians alike, application of modern traffic engineering techniques, mainly within the first ring road, application of road safety measures, and, provision of parking facilities, 3) establishment of computer systems to provide bus management information, including an improved bus-stop program. In addition, bus parking, and maintenance depots, and station terminals for railways, and buses will be constructed, 4) financing of road maintenance works, and equipment, including a road maintenance planning system. Engineering designs, local construction supervision, and administration will be financed by the municipal government, 5) development of environmental monitoring, noise abatement, and emissions control programs, and, 6) strengthening technical skills, agency management and coordination, through technical assistance, training, and consulting services.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Hubei Xiaogan-Xiangfan Highway Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Shijiazhuang Urban Transport Project
long_description: The Shijiazhuang Urban Transport Project will foster the development of an efficient, and environmentally sustainable urban transport system in the city, and provide as well a wider set of commuting choices to its users. The components are: 1) strengthening the primary, and secondary road networks within the second ring, including construction of interchanges, and railway overpasses/underpasses, and, completion, and widening of existing roads, 2) provision of additional facilities for bicycle users, and pedestrians alike, application of modern traffic engineering techniques, mainly within the first ring road, application of road safety measures, and, provision of parking facilities, 3) establishment of computer systems to provide bus management information, including an improved bus-stop program. In addition, bus parking, and maintenance depots, and station terminals for railways, and buses will be constructed, 4) financing of road maintenance works, and equipment, including a road maintenance planning system. donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Second Jiangxi Highway Project
long_description: The Second Jiangxi Highway Project aims at providing more efficient, and safe transport infrastructure in support of social, and economic development in the Province. The components will: 1) expand highway capacity through the construction of the Taihe-Ganzhou Expressway (TGE), partly traversing mountainous areas, to serve Ganzhou, the province second largest city, and, substantially reduce travel distances to Guangdong Province. TGE construction includes tunnels, interconnecting roads, toll stations, and service/rest areas, as well as equipment supply. Furthermore, a management, and maintenance center, and monitoring centers will be included, in addition to electrical/mechanical facilities for telecommunications, lighting, and monitoring activities, 2) include a road improvement program, designed to help people in poor counties, increase their income, through better access to product markets, employment opportunities, social services, and facilities. This program will improve forty roads sections, and eight selected sections located in four counties. Performance indicators include average daily traffic, availability, and cost of bus services, number of days the road is closed/non-available, number of school children, number of people visiting hospitals/clinics, and, increases/decreases in the County per capita income, 3) enable highway safety, through safety measures at roadwork sites, safety audit checklists, and a pilot project to identify hazardous locations, and, 4) restructure institutional framework to adapt to the changing economic, and competitive environment in China. It includes studies on organizational changes, supply of equipment, and, related training programs. Land acquisition will take place, thus resettlement issues are further discussed in the Resettlement Action Plan.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Second Anhui Highway Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: National Railway Project
long_description: The National Railway Project will improve access to transport, strengthen economic growth, and reduce poverty in the northwest of China, while supporting as well efforts to reform China's railways. There are two main components, described as follows. The first component will finance the expansion of a key east-west railway corridor between Baoji, and Lanzhou (Bao-Lan line). This will be achieved by double-tracking, and upgrading the existing line, which includes land acquisition and resettlement, engineering and construction works - bridges, tunnels and culverts - and track installation. Although the Bank will not finance civil works for the Bao-Lan line, it will finance the procurement of rails, turnouts, bridge beams, and goods. Further detailed procurement includes survey and design, equipment for telecommunications, signaling, electrification and power supply, environment, maintenance and operation, and, track maintenance. The accrued interest during construction will be capitalized, to be financed by local funds. The second component will finance studies to support railway reforms, which includes studies on the cost model research of the passenger transportation enterprise, in establishing a wagon-ownership enterprise, and, on tools integration work. Finally, the loan will finance the front-end fee.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Urumqi Urban Transport Improvement Project
long_description: The Urumqi Urban Transport Improvement Project aims to foster a multi-modal urban transport system which is planned, designed, and used for the safe, efficient, and healthy movement of people and goods. The project has five components. 1) Road Network Development will provide a 31.25 km  Ring Road  which will link the old central business district (CBD) to new development areas of the city, protect the CBD from extraneous traffic, and distribute regional traffic to the urban trunk road network. 2) Traffic Management and Road Safety will enhance the efficiency in the use of the existing road space and improve traffic safety. 3) Public Transport will develop public transport as a viable and sustainable travel alternative to the car by improving the level of existing bus services. It will support the ongoing reforms towards the strengthening of the regulatory and planning capabilities of the Urban Construction Commission and support commercial viability of Urumqi Public Transport Company. 4) Environmental Management will support Urumqi's leadership which is very active in promoting and implementing environmental protection and improvement initiatives, and further develop a Motor Vehicle Emission Control Strategy. 5) Institutional Development will strengthen local capacity and processes for urban transport, planning, programming and budgeting, and system management.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Fourth Inland Waterways Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Xinjiang  Highway Project (02)
long_description: The Second Xinjiang Highway Project will build on the achievements of the on-going Xinjiang Highway Project. The project will have the following objectives: a) support road infrastructure to relieve congestion and stimulate socio-economic development in the Urumqui-Kuitun Highway (UKH) corridor, b) improve maintenance of the regional highway network, c) promote development of institutional capacities of Xinjiang Communications Department (XCD) through training and other means, d) increase highway safety, and e) promote transport linkages with neighboring provinces and countries. The scope of the project includes: a) construction of highway between Urumqi and Kuitun, b) construction rehabilitation and upgrading of linking roads to UKH interchanges, c) construction supervision of UKH and linking roads, d) a highway maintenance component, e) equipment procurement, f) institutional strengthening and training of staff of the XCD and related agencies and institutes, g) a highway safety component,and h) a strategic study of regional transport within Xinjiang and between Xinjiang and neighboring provinces and countries.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Hubei Shiman Highway Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: National Highway (02) Project
long_description: The specific objectives of the Second National Highway Project are to: a) relieve road transport congestion and improve the integration of interregional trade and commerce, b) strengthen institutional capacity, c) develop a policy dialogue in the areas of the commercialization and corporatization of provincial expressway companies, highway maintenance management and transport and economic integration, and d) improve the safety of road transport.  The project comprises the following components: a) expressway construction, including service and maintenance facilities and toll plazas, b) interconnecting roads programs, c) highway construction, d) construction supervision services, e) studies in highway maintenance management, expressway commercialization and corporatization, economic integration, and expressway safety, f) staff training programs, and g) equipment.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Fuzhou Nantai Island Peri-Urban Development Project
short_description: FUZHOU NANTAI ISLAND PERI-URBAN DEVELOPMENT PROJECT

title: Fuzhou Nantai Island Peri-Urban Development Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Guiyang Guangzhou Railway

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: NanGuang Railway Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Shaanxi Provincial Highway Project (02)
long_description: The Second Shaanxi Provincial Highway Project has the following objectives: 1) to support continuing development of road infrastructure to relieve congestion, to improve access to important archeological and tourist sites, to facilitate mobility and to increase the efficiency of road transport in the interest of stimulating economic activity by supporting a slice of the Shaanxi highway investment program during the ninth five-year plan, 2) to assist in the implementation of policies to alleviate poverty in the poor areas of the province, 3) to promote development of institutional capabilities of the Shaanxi Provincial Transport Department, through training and other means, in planning, design, operations and maintenance of Shaanxi's highway network, and 4) to increase the safety of road transport. The components of the project are as follows: 1) construction of five high-grade highways, 2) upgrading of the Xian-Sanyuan class I highway, 3) construction, rehabilitation and upgrading of three linking roads, 4) improvement and new road construction for poverty alleviation in poor counties in Shaanxi, 5) provision of equipment for high-grade highways, maintenance of roads and institutional strengthening, 6) road safety program, and 7) institutional strengthening and training.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: GUANGXI URBAN ENVIRONMENT PROJECT
long_description: The Guangxi Urban Environment Project helps improve the environment of Nanning and Guilin, in support of sustainable economic growth and poverty alleviation in the region. It improves water quality, and institutional and financial capacity for environmental protection, environmental services, and water resource management. There are six components. The Sewerage and Drainage component in Nanning cleans up the Chaoyang stream and builds interceptors, builds and improves main and secondary sewers and drains, associated pumping stations, and a primary sewage treatment plant, improves equipment and information systems, and enforces industry regulation. The Sewerage and Solid Waste Management component in Guilin builds primary and secondary sewers, builds pumping stations, expands existing and builds primary sewage treatment plants and oxidation pond, cleans up and intercepts sewage from three lakes, builds a sanitary solid waste landfill, and augments the fleet of solid waste trucks. Component 3 builds or improves tertiary sewers, drains, solid waste dumps, public toilets, and footpaths in selected small areas. Component 4 lines and extends a canal conveying water from a reservoir to the Lijiang, installing a hydro-power generator, and building and repairing embankment sections. Component 5 implments a pilot project to fund sugar refinery pollution abatement. Component 6 supports institutional development, providing technical assistance, training, and equipment.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Second Tianjin Urban Development and Environment Project
short_description: SECOND TIANJIN URBAN DEVELOPMENT AND ENVIRONMENT PROJECT
long_description: The Second Tianjin Urban Development and Environment Project will develop and implement physical, and institutional measures to enhance the efficiency, and equity of urban wastewater management, and transportation systems, thus facilitating Tianjin sustainable development. The project consists of the following components: 1) the environment component includes construction of storm water drains, sanitary sewers, and pumping stations, in Nanbeicang and Fukangnanlu areas, complementing the wastewater treatment plant (WWTP) being financed by the Japan Bank for International Development and the Asia Development Bank, drains and sewers in the south suburb district of Tianjin, construction of a WWTP in Shuanglin, construction of water reclamation plants at the Dongjiao and Shuanglin WWTPs, and reclaimed water distribution systems for industrial/landscape uses, dredging and rehabilitation of cross-sections, bridges and culverts, and pumping stations of the Dagu Canal, and, municipal wastewater collection, and treatment systems in suburban towns of Tianjin municipality, 2) the transportation component entails construction of interchanges at two major intersections of the Middle Ring Road, and low traffic engineering improvements along the Inner Ring Road, extension of the area traffic control system, to include channels, in connection with bus priority measures, and, implementation of bus priority measures, and, 3) technical assistance and training will support the development of infrastructure information systems, of sewerage institutions and finance, of an organization to coordinate urban transport management, and, restructuring of bus routes, and design of bus priority measures.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: POOR RURAL COMMUNITIES DEVELOPMENT PROJECT

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Henan Provincial Highway Project (02)
long_description: The Second Henan Provincial Highway Project has the following objectives: 1) to support continuing development of road infrastructure to relieve congestion, facilitate mobility and increase the efficiency of road transport in the interest of stimulating economic activity, 2) to assist in the implementation of policies to alleviate poverty in the poor areas of the province, 3) to promote development of institutional capabilities of Henan Provincial Communications Department (HPCD), through training and other means, in planning, design, operations and maintenance of Henan's highway network, and 4) to increase the safety of road transport. The project's components include the following: 1) construction of the Luoyang-Sanmenxia Expressway (LSE), which will include supply and installation of electrical, electronic and mechanical equipment for toll collection, telecommunications and lighting facilities, as well as construction of service areas and management and maintenance buildings, 2) construction of the Yangjia-Baijiazhai Connection (YBC) to link the Weinan-Tongguan Expressway (WTE) in Shaanxi Province with the existing Highway 310 in Henan Province, 3) construction, rehabilitation and upgrading of four linking roads that will interconnect and feed traffic to the LSE at interchanges, 4) improvement and new construction of rural roads in a program to alleviate poverty (RIPA) in poor counties in Henan, 5) construction supervision of the LSE, the linking roads and RIPA, 6) provision of equipment, 7) institutional strengthening and training, and 8) a highway safety component to help reduce accidents on Henan's highways.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Qinba Mountains Poverty Reduction Project
long_description: The principal objectives of the Qinba Mountains Poverty Reduction Project are to: (a) demonstrate the effectiveness of a focused multisectoral rural development project approach to poverty reduction, (b) facilitate a  market-friendly  increase in labor mobility from the poor areas to better-off rural and rapidly growing urban areas, (c) upgrade poverty monitoring at the national and local levels, and (d) significantly reduce absolute poverty in 26 of the very poorest counties in the Qinba mountains region.  The project will also stabilize or even reverse upland environmental destruction through land and pasture improvement and soil conservation works, and encourage greater local community participation by enabling households to make key decisions during project design and implementation.  The project will comprise the following six components: 1) labor mobility, 2) rural infrastructure, 3) land and farmer development, 4) township and village enterprise (TVE) development, 5) microfinance, and 6) institution building and poverty monitoring.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Third National Railway Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Liaoning Medium Cities Infrastructure Project

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: Guangzhou City Center Transport Project
long_description: The Guangzhou City Center Transport Project improves access to the city center of Guangzhou by promoting the efficient use of the urban transport system in an environmentally sustainable way. There are six project components. Component 1, the Inner Ring Road (GIRR) (construction), acquires land and resettles project-affected people as an urban development program. Component 2, Traffic Management and Safety, develops a bus lane network, provides footpaths and sidewalks, segregates bicycle routes, provides technical assistance, uses Geographic Information Systems (GIS) to improve traffic management analysis and planning, installs and improves traffic signs and computerized signals, improves road safety by updating accident management computer software, monitors, manages, and integrates GIRR traffic control equipment, and invests in parking control management. Component 3 supports institutional reform of public transport, constructs a maintenance depot, provides equipment, and tests selected buses. Component 4, the Vehicle Emissions Control Program, introduces unleaded gasoline, inspects and maintains vehicles, improves the automated motor vehicle pollution monitoring system, and sets up the Vehicle Emission Research Center. Component 5 develops a road maintenance system. Component 6 strengthens institutional capacity for planning, administration, and management of public programs with training, research, and consultants.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: China Container Transport Project
long_description: The Container Transport Project aims at facilitating inland penetration of seaborne containers from gateway ports to inland cities, thus contributing to reducing economic disparities between coastal and inland areas. There are two main components in this project. First, Inland Container Depot (ICD) development aims at developing container handling capability at selected inland cities located along two pilot corridors. The Bank will finance qualified ICDs for their construction and the procurement of equipment. Second, upgrading Tianjin Port container handling capacity will extend financial assistance to the upgrading of container handling equipment at Tianjin Harbor Container Company, and providing technical assistance to establish a better operational and institutional environment for container operations.

donor: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
agency: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
title: National Highway Project (04)
long_description: The Fourth National Highway Project for China aims to increase economic activity along the Wuhan-Changsha high-priority transport corridor through relieving congestion, facilitating trade and mobility, and increasing efficiency and traffic safety in Northern Hunan and Southern Hubei. There are four project components. The first has several subcomponents including construction of two expressway sections, building of interconnecting and other roads, supplying equipment to control construction quality and environmental monitoring, to operate and monitor the new expressway sections, and maintain the provincial road network, and construction supervision services. The second component funds studies and technical assistance in highway maintenance management, transport and trade integration, expressway commercialization and corporatization, and road safety as well as staff training programs covering all aspects of highway planning, design, construction, operation, finance, and maintenance. The third component funds the front-end fee while the fourth component finances land acquisition and resettlement.
