title: Accelerating Sustainable Public-Private Investment for Infrastructure Renewal (ASPIRE) / Accroissement des investissements public privé durables pour
short_description: ACCELERATING SUSTAINABLE PUBLIC-PRIVATE INVESTMENT FOR INFRASTRUCTURE RENEWAL (ASPIRE) / ACCROISSEMENT DES INVESTISSEMENTS PUBLIC PRIVÉ DURABLES POUR
long_description: This initiative aims to facilitate the preparation and structuring of bankable Public Private Partnership (PPP) projects to increase investment in infrastructure to support sustainable economic growth in Indonesia. The project includes two interrelated components. The first aims to establish and operationalize a PPP Centre to act as the country's lead agency in PPP project development. The second provides critical complementary support to develop a strong PPP project pipeline, lay the legal and regulatory framework for PPPs in infrastructure, and catalyze early bankable PPP projects. These early investments are intended to demonstrate the value of the PPPs in addressing sustainable infrastructure for poverty reduction and increased access to markets, with an emphasis on the transportation sector, an area of strong Canadian expertise. The project also allows the Indonesia PPP Centre to receive support from the APEC PPP Expert Advisory Panel and to guide the development of PPPs projects in Asia. / Cette initiative vise à faciliter la préparation et le montage de projets de partenariats publics-privés (PPP) susceptibles de bénéficier d'un concours financier pour accroitre l'investissement dans l'infrastructure pour appuyer la croissance économique durable en Indonésie. Le projet comprend deux composantes interreliées. La première vise à établir un centre des PPP et à le mettre en service pour agir à titre d'agence principale du pays dans l'élaboration de projets de PPP. La deuxième fournit un soutien complémentaire essentiel pour développer une solide filière de projets de PPP, élaborer le cadre juridique et réglementaire pour les PPP dans l'infrastructure, et jouer un rôle de catalyseur dans les premiers projets de PPP susceptibles de bénéficier d'un concours financier. Ces premiers investissements sont destinés à démontrer la valeur des PPP lorsqu'il s'agit de prendre des mesures à l'égard de l'infrastructure durable pour la réduction de la pauvreté et un accès accru aux marchés, en mettant l'accent sur le secteur des transports, un domaine où le Canada possède une grande expertise. Le projet permet également au Centre de PPP de l'Indonésie de recevoir du soutien du Comité consultatif d'experts de l'APEC sur les PPP et de guider l'élaboration de projets de PPP en Asie.

title: Private Sector Engagement for Agricultural Development / Participation du secteur privé pour le développement agricole
short_description: PRIVATE SECTOR ENGAGEMENT FOR AGRICULTURAL DEVELOPMENT / PARTICIPATION DU SECTEUR PRIVÉ POUR LE DÉVELOPPEMENT AGRICOLE
long_description: The project aims to improve sustainable rural growth for 7,500 farmers in the Mekong delta and Central Highlands of Vietnam. The project contributes to the Government of Vietnam's reform efforts in sustainable economic growth and works to increase competitiveness in the agriculture sector by engaging the private sector.   The project focuses on: (i) increasing lending for agricultural activities by private sector banks to give farmers the means to invest in the most profitable seeds, techniques and storage equipment, and to give them the flexibility to wait for the ideal time to sell their goods at better prices, (ii) providing training to farmers to help them improve their agricultural practices, by diversifying their income streams, and improving their financial management, for example, and (iii) improving the handling and storage of crops by farmers, agribusinesses and warehouses to ensure the quality, quantity, and market value of the crops is maintained. The project makes a particular effort to ensure that women farmers have equitable access to financial services and training.   Some project activities include: (i) advising up to five private sector banks already engaged in agricultural lending to help them increase their lending, develop appropriate financial products for selected markets, including tailored products for women farmers, (ii) providing training of trainers to 300 people (40 percent women) on how to change farming practices to mitigate risks such as climate, pests, price shock, and marketing, and (iii) supporting agribusinesses to undertake risk analysis of post-harvest losses in order to design tailored solutions for farmers, and training 200 trainers to deliver training on these solutions to farmer groups. / Le projet profitera à 7 500 agriculteurs du delta du Mékong et des hautes terres centrales du Vietnam. Ce projet vise à contribuer aux efforts de réforme réalisés par le gouvernement du Vietnam en matière de croissance économique durable et vise à accroitre la compétitivité dans le secteur de l'agriculture par la participation du secteur privé.   Le projet met l'accent sur : i) l'augmentation du nombre de prêts octroyés au secteur agricole par les banques du secteur privé afin de donner aux agriculteurs les moyens d'investir dans les semences, les techniques et l'équipement de stockage les plus rentables, et de leur donner la possibilité d'attendre le moment idéal pour vendre leurs produits à meilleur prix, ii) la formation des agriculteurs en matière de pratiques agricoles améliorées, en diversifiant leurs sources de revenus et en améliorant leur gestion financière, par exemple, et iii) l'amélioration de la manutention et de l'entreposage des récoltes des agriculteurs, des entreprises agricoles et des entrepôts afin d'assurer le maintien de la qualité, quantité et valeur de marché des cultures. Le projet fait un effort particulier pour assurer que les femmes agriculteurs aient un accès équitable aux services financiers

purpose: Education and training in transport and storage

title: Asia Pacific Project Preparation Facility / Facilité de préparation de projet Asie Pacifique
short_description: ASIA PACIFIC PROJECT PREPARATION FACILITY / FACILITÉ DE PRÉPARATION DE PROJET ASIE PACIFIQUE
long_description: The project aims to address major, pressing infrastructure needs in the Asia Pacific region by facilitating increased private sector investment in infrastructure projects, mainly through public-private partnerships (PPPs). Project activities include: (1) providing financial, legal and technical assistance services to governments and their public sector agencies to better prepare, structure and tender infrastructure projects, (2) supporting legal and regulatory reforms that facilitate private sector investments in infrastructure projects.     Increased private sector participation in infrastructure improves access to technology, markets and finance. The Facility helps to address the need for additional infrastructure services, a necessity that the public sector cannot meet on its own. Better infrastructure stands to benefit local populations, increasing access to key basic services (i.e.: water and sanitation), and linking producers to markets, creating job opportunities, and connecting Asia Pacific countries to the global economy. / Le projet vise à répondre à des besoins majeurs et pressants en infrastructures dans la région de l'Asie-Pacifique en facilitant les investissements du secteur privé dans les projets d'infrastructure, principalement par l'intermédiaire de partenariats public-privé. Parmi les activités du projet : 1) fournir des services financiers, juridiques et de soutien technique aux gouvernements et aux organismes gouvernementaux de façon à mieux préparer et structurer les projets d'infrastructure et à améliorer les appels d'offres, 2) appuyer les réformes juridiques et de la réglementation qui facilitent les investissements du secteur privé dans des projets d'infrastructure.   Une participation accrue du secteur privé dans les projets d'infrastructure améliore l'accès aux technologies, aux marchés et au financement. La Facilité contribue à répondre au besoin pour des services d'infrastructure additionnels, besoin que le secteur public ne peut pas satisfaire à lui seul. Les populations locales profiteront de l'amélioration des infrastructures puisque celles-ci faciliteront l'accès aux services de base importants (l'eau et l'assainissement, p. ex.), relieront les producteurs aux marchés, contribueront à la création d'emploi et permettront de relier les pays de l'Asie-Pacifique à l'économie mondiale.

The project also allows the Indonesia PPP Centre to receive support from the APEC PPP Expert Advisory Panel and to guide the development of PPPs projects in Asia. / Cette initiative vise à faciliter la préparation et le montage de projets de partenariats publics-privés (PPP) susceptibles de bénéficier d'un concours financier pour accroitre l'investissement dans l'infrastructure pour appuyer la croissance économique durable en Indonésie. Le projet comprend deux composantes interreliées. La première vise à établir un centre des PPP et à le mettre en service pour agir à titre d'agence principale du pays dans l'élaboration de projets de PPP. La deuxième fournit un soutien complémentaire essentiel pour développer une solide filière de projets de PPP, élaborer le cadre juridique et réglementaire pour les PPP dans l'infrastructure, et jouer un rôle de catalyseur dans les premiers projets de PPP susceptibles de bénéficier d'un concours financier. Ces premiers investissements sont destinés à démontrer la valeur des PPP lorsqu'il s'agit de prendre des mesures à l'égard de l'infrastructure durable pour la réduction de la pauvreté et un accès accru aux marchés, en mettant l'accent sur le secteur des transports, un domaine où le Canada possède une grande expertise. Le projet permet également au Centre de PPP de l'Indonésie de recevoir du soutien du Comité consultatif d'experts de l'APEC sur les PPP et de guider l'élaboration de projets de PPP en Asie.

title: Technical Assistance - U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program Phase XI, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM PHASE XI, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: In fiscal year 2015, USTDA funding of $530,401 continued support for the U.S. private sector-led U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). Continuation of the program ensured an ongoing high-level dialogue with the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC). This training and technical assistance aimed to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies to gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

title: Technical Symposium - 2016 U.S.-China Aviation Symposium, through American Association of Airport Executives
short_description: TECHNICAL SYMPOSIUM - 2016 U.S.-CHINA AVIATION SYMPOSIUM, THROUGH AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF AIRPORT EXECUTIVES
long_description: USTDA funding of $477,711 supported the 2016 U.S.-China Aviation Symposium, a bi-annual technical symposium (TS) held in the United States, followed by site visits. This symposium provided an excellent opportunity for senior U.S. and Chinese government officials and private sector aviation leaders to engage in substantive discussions on priority topics such as aviation sustainability, general aviation, airports and security, weather, managing the supply chain, new aviation technologies, and air traffic management, safety, efficiency, and capacity. The purpose of this TS was to bring procurement officials to the United States to witness U.S. technology and ingenuity firsthand and develop the relationships with U.S. companies necessary to spur increased exports.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program Phase X, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM PHASE X, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA continued support for the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). This strategic, multi-program, public-private partnership that USTDA launched in 2004 has produced significant developmental and U.S. export results. Continuation of the program ensured an ongoing high-level dialogue with the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC), which was intended to promote the interests of the U.S. aviation sector in China.

title: Technical Assistance - Airport Geographic Information System, through Hi-Tec Systems, Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AIRPORT GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION SYSTEM, THROUGH HI-TEC SYSTEMS, INC.
long_description: This grant is supporting technical assistance (TA) for the Airports Authority of India (AAI) to develop and test a prototype Airport Geographic Information System (AGIS). AAI and Hi-Tec Systems, Inc. jointly proposed this sole-source project to USTDA as a priority for the U.S.-India Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). AGIS can store aeronautical geographic information in an electronic format, which can be updated, edited, and shared within AAI as well as with other airports, regulators, and contractors in India's civil aviation realm, increasing overall sector efficiencies. The TA project would ultimately develop a prototype system for one medium-sized airport in India as a proof of concept for future customization, deployment, and application of AGIS technologies for other airports in the country. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Technical Assistance - Ground Based Augmentation System Pilot Project, through Honeywell International, Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - GROUND BASED AUGMENTATION SYSTEM PILOT PROJECT, THROUGH HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC.
long_description: This $407,325 grant supported a technical assistance (TA) to the Airports Authority of India (AAI) for the installation, certification, and operational commissioning of a Ground Based Augmentation System (GBAS) from Honeywell at Chennai International Airport. The implementation and operational service approval of GBAS in India would improve air navigation and airport approach procedures, and thus create greater fuel efficiencies for air carriers as well as safety and capacity improvements at major airports in the country. This TA aimed to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Transport Services
long_description: Support and strengthen reliable and affordable transport systems.

short_description: CIVIL AVIATION SUSTAINABILITY GREEN ROUTES INITIATIVE, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA is funding $871,050 in technical assistance (TA) to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) for the U.S.-China Aviation Sustainability Green Routes Initiative program in support of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). This TA is designed to promote the implementation of sustainable practices in the aviation sector and assess the feasibility of expanding the concept from one single test pilot flight to multiple flights and city pairs. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

long_description: USTDA funding had enabled the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for USTDA-funded activities supporting the twelfth phase of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP), including the eleventh phase of the Executive Management Development Training (EMDT) Program and the eighth Air Traffic Management Executive Training (ATMET). This training and technical assistance aimed to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

short_description: AVIATION TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE WORKSHOPS PHASE II FAA TRANSFER FUNDS, THROUGH U.S. GOVERNMENT - DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
long_description: USTDA funding is enabling the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for the USTDA-funded China Aviation Technical Assistance (ATA) Workshops. The ATA Workshops aim to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

This training and technical assistance aimed to strengthen the efficiency and safety of China's aviation system, while assisting U.S. companies to gain market entry into the Chinese aviation sector.

title: Technical Assistance - Aviation Safety Phase II, through Wicks Group, PLLC
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AVIATION SAFETY PHASE II, THROUGH WICKS GROUP, PLLC
long_description: This $808,327 technical assistance (TA) grant funds an Aviation Safety Project (ASP) Phase II for India's Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA). The TA is assisting the DGCA in implementing additional improvements to its procedures, requirements and guidance, continuing to develop an appropriately-sized qualified inspectorate staff, completing further air carrier certifications, accomplishing aviation training organization certifications, and new oversight activities. The TA is also assisting in the recommendation of certification and licensing procedural changes that would better support business and general aviation growth in India, while also enabling appropriate oversight and monitoring of this aviation sub-sector. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation..

title: Technical Assistance - U.S.-China Aviation Sustainability Initiative, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - U.S.-CHINA AVIATION SUSTAINABILITY INITIATIVE, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA is providing a $885,700 technical assistance (TA) to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) for the U.S.-China Aviation Sustainability Initiative program in support of the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). This program is assisting CAAC in developing a sustainable aviation plan to help reduce emissions and energy consumption associated with the country's growing aviation industry. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Reverse Trade Mission - Third Istanbul Airport, through American Association of Airport Executives
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - THIRD ISTANBUL AIRPORT, THROUGH AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF AIRPORT EXECUTIVES
long_description: This reverse trade mission provided the opportunity for twelve high-ranking Turkish officials and executives to meet with U.S. companies that offer aviation and airport technology solutions. The delegates also met with U.S. airport executives who have faced similar airport infrastructure challenges and have implemented the latest innovations to ensure the most efficient airport operations and the highest level of airport capacity.

title: Advancing Philippine Competitiveness (COMPETE) Project - Transport Services
short_description: ADVANCING PHILIPPINE COMPETITIVENESS (COMPETE) PROJECT - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: The Advancing Philippine Competitiveness (COMPETE) Project is a four-year, Php774.5 million ($18.9 million) project began in October 2012. COMPETE assists the Philippines to improve its competitiveness to attain higher levels of trade and investment. To this end, the Project will provide technical assistance to enhance the regime for infrastructure provision, improve productivity in key industries, and increase access to credit. COMPETE is a USAID/Philippines project under the Partnership for Growth (PFG), a White House initiative. The PFG represents a partnership between the Philippines and the United States to promote broad-based and inclusive growth. The U.S. Government is working with the Government of the Philippines, the private sector and civil society organizations to help put the Philippines on an accelerated growth trajectory that benefits the majority of its population.

title: Strengthening Urban Resilience for Growth with Equity (SURGE) - Transport Services
short_description: STRENGTHENING URBAN RESILIENCE FOR GROWTH WITH EQUITY (SURGE) - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: Strengthening Urban Resilience for Growth with Equity (SURGE) is a crucial component of Partnership for Growth (PFG) and the Cities Development Initiative (CDI). The primary goal is to support a highly innovative, creative, and cost efficient solutions that set the conditions for broad-based and inclusive and resilient economic growth and foster higher investment, increased economic opportunities and productive employment for a critical mass of cities and regions outside Metro Manila. The SURGE activity aims to promote more balanced and resilient urban growth, reduce economic disparities, and improve socio-economic conditions for highly urbanized second-tier cities and their surrounding areas. This activity will assist cities and adjacent areas plan effectively, guarantee basic public services, reduce business transaction costs, promote competitiveness, support sustainable development, and reduce disaster risks while ensuring inclusive and sustainable growth. Interventions will promote efforts to: a. Improve local capacity in urban developmentb.Increase local economic development by fostering business enabling measuresc.

title: Flight Delay Management Technical Assistance Program - Phase II Faa Transfer Funds, through U.S. Government - Department of Transportation
short_description: FLIGHT DELAY MANAGEMENT TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM - PHASE II FAA TRANSFER FUNDS, THROUGH U.S. GOVERNMENT - DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
long_description: USTDA funding of $115,828 is enabling the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration to deliver training and program support for USTDA-funded activities under the Flight Delay Management Technical Assistance (TA) Program, which is a U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP) activity. The training and technical assistance aim to build strong relationships between U.S. and Chinese aviation sectors and ensure that the Chinese aviation remains open for U.S. exports as the market continues to expand.

title: Feasibility Study - Green Port Communications Pilot Project, through ARES Security Corporation
short_description: FEASIBILITY STUDY - GREEN PORT COMMUNICATIONS PILOT PROJECT, THROUGH ARES SECURITY CORPORATION
long_description: This $843,720 grant funds a pilot project and feasibility study to assist the China Waterborne Transport Research Institute with assessing the ability of U.S. technology to reduce the environmental impact of port operations at the Port of Qinghuangdao. The pilot project and feasibility study are demonstrating the capabilities of U.S. software developed by ARES Security Corporation and environmental monitoring systems developed by its partner, Triad Engineering Applications.

title: Feasibility Study - Integrated Telecommunications Control Center – Ho Chi Minh City Metro, through Cisco Systems, Inc.
short_description: FEASIBILITY STUDY - INTEGRATED TELECOMMUNICATIONS CONTROL CENTER – HO CHI MINH CITY METRO, THROUGH CISCO SYSTEMS, INC.
long_description: This $900,000 feasibility study is supporting the establishment of an Integrated Telecommunications Control Center for the purpose of developing information and communications technology (ICT) infrastructure for the metro rail system in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. This study aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation Air Traffic Management And Air Traffic Control Technical Workshop, through The Webster Group
short_description: ASIA-PACIFIC ECONOMIC COOPERATION AIR TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT AND AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL TECHNICAL WORKSHOP, THROUGH THE WEBSTER GROUP
long_description: USTDA funded a one-day regional Air Traffic Management (ATM) and Air Traffic Control (ATC) technical workshop in the Philippines. The activity was designed to disseminate the findings associated with a related activity, the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) Air Traffic Management Emissions Reduction Technical Assistance (TA). The workshop included: a) an overview session on ATM, Collaborative Decision Making (CDM), and other ATM/ATC concepts and the corresponding benefits of these technologies, and b) a detailed overview of the TA that the contractor performed to disseminate information to the entire APEC membership regarding the benefits of ATM and CDM tools and potential implementation methods, using the airspace in and between Vietnam and the Philippines as an example. The purpose of this workshop was to introduce procurement officials to U.S. technology and ingenuity firsthand and develop the relationships with U.S. companies necessary to spur increased exports.

title: Desk Study - Xian Flight Delay Management, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: DESK STUDY - XIAN FLIGHT DELAY MANAGEMENT, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA funding of $1,022,404 supported a Flight Delay Management Technical Assistance Program provided to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC). The project, developed in close cooperation with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), assisted CAAC's Air Traffic Management Bureau (ATMB) to develop efficient macro-level air traffic management principles as well as improve specific processes at two of the country's largest airports: Beijing and Guangzhou. This technical assistance program aimed to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Transport Services
long_description: Support and strengthen reliable and affordable transport systems.

title: Reverse Trade Mission - Civil Aviation, through American Association of Airport Executives
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - CIVIL AVIATION, THROUGH AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF AIRPORT EXECUTIVES
long_description: This New Istanbul Airport II Reverse Trade Mission will bring a delegation from the Turkish civil aviation sector to the United States to gain exposure to advanced air traffic management, as well as aviation security technology, services and best practices through visits to U.S. facilities and meetings with leading U.S. companies. The purpose of this RTM is to bring procurement officials to the United States to witness U.S. technology and ingenuity firsthand and develop the relationships with U.S. companies necessary to spur increased exports.

title: Construction Monitoring and Evaluation Program (CMEP) - Transport Services
short_description: CONSTRUCTION MONITORING AND EVALUATION PROGRAM (CMEP) - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: The Province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa suffered heavy infrastructure damages firstly due to terrorism and militancy and thereafter due to the unprecedented floods of July 2010. United States Agency for International Development (USAID) actively supported the rehabilitation and reconstruction activities of damaged schools, health facilities, drinking water supply schemes and other infrastructure components in Malakand Division by initially obligating funds amounting to USD 36 million under an Activity Agreement (No. 391-011) signed in September, 2009 between USAID and PaRRSA on behalf of GoP. Expanding its portfolio, USAID provided funds for rehabilitation / reconstruction of other infrastructure components including irrigation, bridges and roads etc. in the calamity hit areas. In June 2012, USAID sub-obligated additional funds for reconstruction of rains / floods damaged and bomb blasted schools in the affected districts of KP under Amended Activity Agreement No. 391-014. M/ AGES Consultants was assigned the task of Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) services of these reconstruction activities under USAID/Pakistan Construction Monitoring and Evaluation Program (CMEP), for which a formal contract was signed on September 30, 2012.

title: Peshawar  Torkham Road Rehabilitation - Transport Services
short_description: PESHAWAR  TORKHAM ROAD REHABILITATION - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: The PeshawarTorkham road project is intended to: Improve local economic opportunities, Allow internal and international trade to flow more easily, Offer employment opportunities for local workers and Extend and support the delivery of basic infrastructure services by the Government of Pakistan.

title: Improvements to the rehabilitated Kalat-Quetta-Chaman Highway - Transport Services
short_description: IMPROVEMENTS TO THE REHABILITATED KALAT-QUETTA-CHAMAN HIGHWAY - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) provided funding to for further improvements of the rehabilitated Kalat-Quetta-Chaman Highway, including a bypass road at Kuchlak, four bridges, two weighing stations, and three toll plazas.

long_description: Principal Repayments: USAID - Country Loans [Republic of Indonesia]

short_description: TRADE-RELATED TRAINING PROGRAM - AIRCRAFT RESCURE AND FIREFIGHTING TRAINING PROJECT, THROUGH OSHKOSH AIRPORT PRODUCTS, LLC
long_description: This $364,000 training grant to the Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) supported the training of representatives from Philippine airports through advanced firefighter and emergency response training as well as driver and operations training. Oshkosh Airport Products, LLC provided the majority of the training on-site in the Philippines, and also provided one week of training to senior CAAP officials in Oshkosh, WI at their headquarters.

title: Definitional Mission - Railways And Ports Infrastructure Development, through TERA International Group, Inc.
short_description: DEFINITIONAL MISSION - RAILWAYS AND PORTS INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT, THROUGH TERA INTERNATIONAL GROUP, INC.
long_description: This definitional mission (DM) is identifying, evaluating and recommending opportunities related to the rail, ports and Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) sectors in Turkey for USTDA funding consideration. The DM contractor is assessing prospective transportation infrastructure development projects, particularly in the rail and ports sectors, but also for Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), which would advance economic development in Turkey and the region, and have the potential for U.S. exports to Turkey.

title: REBUILD following Typhoon Yolanda - Transport Services
short_description: REBUILD FOLLOWING TYPHOON YOLANDA - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: Super Typhoon Haiyan (known in the Philippines as Yolanda) made landfall on November 8, 2013, killing more than 6,000 people and affecting 16 million more. Most of the public infrastructure across the central Philippine islands was demolished. The storm destroyed nearly 90% of the homes in Tacloban, the largest city in the affected area with a pre-storm population of 220,000.

title: Technical Assistance - Rail Information Technology Integration and Development, through Strategic Rail, LLC
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - RAIL INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATION AND DEVELOPMENT, THROUGH STRATEGIC RAIL, LLC
long_description: This $437,000 grant to the national railway company of Kazakhstan, Kazakhstan Temir Zholy JSC (KTZ), supports technical assistance (TA) to implement modern information technology (IT) systems. This TA is helping KTZ consolidate its 14 subsidiaries into three departments covering freight, passenger, and infrastructure. By improving IT systems, KTZ will be able to improve operational efficiency and provide real-time data to its senior management that will aid in improving safety. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Technical Assistance - Air Simulation For The New Istanbul Airport, through Jeppesen Sanderson, Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AIR SIMULATION FOR THE NEW ISTANBUL AIRPORT, THROUGH JEPPESEN SANDERSON, INC.
long_description: This $1,061,314 technical assistance grant is funding and supporting the design of the optimal airspace, runway approaches and air traffic control operating procedures for the New Istanbul Airport. The New Istanbul Airport is scheduled to open in 2018 and when completed in 2024, it will be one of the largest in the world, hosting 150 million passengers annually on flights to more than 350 destinations. Accordingly, ?GA Havaliman? I?letmesi A.?. (IGA), the concessionaire, is seeking technical assistance to ensure that the airspace operates at sufficient capacity to meet the anticipated traffic demand on the opening day in 2018. This technical assistance aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Technical Assistance - General Aviation And Business Aviation, through Enterprise - United States Unknown

long_description: This $536,442 technical assistance (TA) to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) funds the General Aviation Development Implementation Support Project in cooperation with the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). This TA is assisting CAAC in its efforts to open the operation of low altitude airspace and develop an expanded general aviation sector in China. This technical assistance aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Program Support (Infrastructure)
long_description: Develop and conduct needs assessments, baseline studies, targeted evaluations, special studies or other information-gathering efforts specifically for the design, monitoring and evaluation of USG-funded programs. This sub-element may also include developing and disseminating best practices and lessons learned, testing demonstration and/or pilot models, or the preparation of strategic plans and other short-term programming tasks. Note: All such activities that are carried out by partners as an integral part of their monitoring and evaluation efforts for programs funded under other sub-elements should be included within those sub-elements.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program Phase XII, through Montgomery Consulting Group, Inc.
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM PHASE XII, THROUGH MONTGOMERY CONSULTING GROUP, INC.
long_description: This desk study reviewed a proposal for a twelfth phase (Phase XII) of technical assistance to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) under the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). The ACP had requested USTDA support for strategic assistance to provide training to China's civil aviation personnel and leaders in priority areas, including air traffic flow management and executive management development.

title: Technical Assistance - Flight Delay Management, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - FLIGHT DELAY MANAGEMENT, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA funding of a $1,113,130 technical assistance (TA) to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) developed in close cooperation with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), is assisting CAAC's Air Traffic Management Bureau (ATMB) to develop a systematic approach to assess factors that influence operational efficiency and capacity at the local, regional, and national system levels, as well as to integrate airport planning with air carrier operations and air traffic procedures. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Kalat - Quetta - Chaman Road Project (Section 2 & 4) - Transport Services
short_description: KALAT - QUETTA - CHAMAN ROAD PROJECT (SECTION 2 & 4) - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: National Highway Authority (NHA), Ministry of Communication and Works, Government of Pakistan (GoP) initially started widening and improvement of KQC road (N-25) in 2006 with ADB Loan No. 2019. For execution purpose, the road was divided into four sections namely, (1) Kalat - Khad Kocha section, (2) Khad Kocha - Quetta section, (3) Quetta - Jungle Piralizai section, and (4) Jungle Piralizai - Chaman section. Sections 1 and 3 (covering 120 km) were completed in 2010. The remaining sections (2 and 4) covering 111 km could only partially be completed due to worst law and order situation and subsequent expiry of ADB loan. With an assurance of funding of US$ 90 million by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) in October, 2013, NHA assigned the balance / leftover works in sections 2 and 4 to M/s Frontier Works Organization (FWO) on EPC Lump sum basis in March 2014 for which formal contract agreement between NHA and FWO was signed on June 02, 2014.

title: FATA Infrastructure Project (FIP) - Transport Services
long_description: The FATA Infrastructure Project (FIP) constructs roads to connect remote communities to markets and facilitate access to essential services, restores electricity infrastructure, and improves access to water for drinking and farming. This program is also supporting the construction of the Kalat-Quetta-Chaman road in Balochistan, an important cross-border trade route between Pakistan and Afghanistan.

title: Technical Assistance - General Aviation and Business Aviation Development Project, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - GENERAL AVIATION AND BUSINESS AVIATION DEVELOPMENT PROJECT, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA funding of $1,053,030 is supporting a General Aviation Development Implementation Support Project in cooperation with the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP). This project is assisting the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) in its efforts to open the operation of low altitude airspace and develop an expanded general aviation sector in China, including helicopters, seaplanes, and air traffic management technologies. This program aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Airport Winter Operations Planning, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: AIRPORT WINTER OPERATIONS PLANNING, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: This $507,969 technical assistance (TA) to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) is supporting the development of winter operations planning at the Harbin International Airport in northeastern China. This TA is intended to identify equipment, facilities, procedures and training solutions to mitigate flight delays and airport closures during extreme winter events. The TA will introduce CAAC to life-cycle costing concepts related to the procurement of ice and snow removal equipment. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Technical Assistance - Aviation Technical Assistance (Ata) Workshop Series Phase II, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AVIATION TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE (ATA) WORKSHOP SERIES PHASE II, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: This $360,000 technical assistance (TA) provided to the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) is supporting a second series of aviation related workshops that will provide a broad range of managerial, technical, and operational training to key officials from CAAC, airport management groups, and individual airports in China. The workshops are intended to serve as a forum where representatives from U.S. industry and government engage Chinese counterparts to collaborate on best practices, and standards and policy development for the aviation industry.

title: U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: U.S.-CHINA AVIATION COOPERATION PROGRAM, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: USTDA continues to support the U.S.-China Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP) with a twelfth phase. This strategic, multi-program, public-private partnership has produced significant developmental and U.S. export results. Continuation of the program ensured an ongoing high-level dialogue with the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC), which was intended to promote the interests of the U.S. aviation sector in China. ACP 2016 includes two components from previous phases that have been highly successful - the Executive Management Development Training (EMDT) and Air Traffic Management Executive Training (ATMET) programs.

title: Eastern Airspace Aviation Safety, through Enterprise - United States Unknown
short_description: EASTERN AIRSPACE AVIATION SAFETY, THROUGH ENTERPRISE - UNITED STATES UNKNOWN
long_description: This $718,000 Eastern Airspace Aviation Safety technical assistance grant is funding the development of a Concept of Operations (ConOps) for the Directorate General of Civil Aviation of Indonesia (DGCA) to help improve the safety of flight and quality of air navigation in Papua and Maluku. The intended outcome of the ConOps is to provide a recommended implementation guideline for the Papua and Maluku airspace and aviation safety program goals and objectives, required capabilities, and the changes needed to realize the safety objectives. Over the long term the ConOps is designed to help increase aviation sector efficiencies, diminish operational inconsistencies in systems, reduce cost, and increase safety of the sector.

title: Reverse Trade Mission - Green Construction And Waste Recycling, through Global Marketing & Communications, Inc.
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - GREEN CONSTRUCTION AND WASTE RECYCLING, THROUGH GLOBAL MARKETING & COMMUNICATIONS, INC.
long_description: This funding supported a Green Construction and Waste Recycling Reverse Trade Mission (RTM) that took place in May 2016. For this RTM, delegates from Chinese construction companies and associations and one U.S. Embassy representative were invited to the United States for site visits and meetings with public and private sector experts on construction waste recycling technologies. The purpose of this RTM was to bring procurement officials to the United States to witness U.S. technology and ingenuity firsthand and develop the relationships with U.S. companies necessary to spur increased exports.

title: Port Hazmat Tracking & Monitoring Project, through Pythia International, Inc.
short_description: PORT HAZMAT TRACKING & MONITORING PROJECT, THROUGH PYTHIA INTERNATIONAL, INC.
long_description: This desk study is evaluating a proposal for a feasibility study and pilot project on an environmental emergency management and response system (EMRS) for the Port of Tianjin and its nearby chemical parks. The proposed project is expected to utilize environmental EMRS capabilities to mitigate risks associated with the transport and storage of chemicals, as well as act as a model for establishing similar systems in other ports across China.

title: Civil Aviation Sustainability Pilot Project, through Montgomery Consulting Group, Inc.
short_description: CIVIL AVIATION SUSTAINABILITY PILOT PROJECT, THROUGH MONTGOMERY CONSULTING GROUP, INC.
long_description: This desk study reviewed a proposal and Terms of Reference for the Civil Aviation Sustainability Pilot Project for the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC).

title: Reverse Trade Mission - Asia-Pacific Flight Standards, through Global Marketing & Communications, Inc.
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - ASIA-PACIFIC FLIGHT STANDARDS, THROUGH GLOBAL MARKETING & COMMUNICATIONS, INC.
long_description: This Asia-Pacific Flight Standards Reverse Trade Mission (RTM) provided an opportunity for seventeen delegates from the Asia-Pacific region to travel to the U.S. to learn about regulatory best practices to improve civil aviation safety and security oversight in their countries. This RTM intended to help the civil aviation authorities bring flight standards into conformance with International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) guidelines and assist the countries in their efforts to attain, regain and/or maintain Category One (Cat I) status under the Federal Aviation Administration's (FAA) International Aviation Safety Assessment (IASA) Program. The purpose of this RTM was to bring procurement officials to the United States to witness U.S. technology and ingenuity firsthand and develop the relationships with U.S. companies necessary to spur increased exports.

title: Reverse Trade Mission - Rail Maintenance Systems, through Enterprise - United States Unknown
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - RAIL MAINTENANCE SYSTEMS, THROUGH ENTERPRISE - UNITED STATES UNKNOWN
long_description: This grant funds a Rail Maintenance Systems technical assistance (TA) to support PT Kereta Api Indonesia (Persero) (PT KAI), a state-owned railway company in Indonesia, in its efforts to develop a comprehensive rail maintenance program. This TA includes a review of the railway's track maintenance practices and standards and recommendations for changes that could result in general improvement in track condition, increases in maximum train speed, and improved safety. This technical assistance aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Technical Assistance - Aviation Security Equipment Testing and Evaluation Program, through Hi-Tec Systems, Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AVIATION SECURITY EQUIPMENT TESTING AND EVALUATION PROGRAM, THROUGH HI-TEC SYSTEMS, INC.
long_description: This $658,000 grant funded technical assistance (TA) to the Airports Authority of India (AAI) and India's Bureau of Civil Aviation Security (BCAS) to establish an Aviation Security Equipment Testing and Evaluation Program (ASETEP). The ASETEP initiative included the development of clear standards, specifications, and testing procedures that align with international best practices for three types of aviation security equipment that scan carry-on luggage, checked baggage, and passengers. This TA aimed to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Indonesia Air Traffic Management And Airport Infrastructure And Security, through Futron Corporation
short_description: INDONESIA AIR TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT AND AIRPORT INFRASTRUCTURE AND SECURITY, THROUGH FUTRON CORPORATION
long_description: This Air Traffic Management and Airport Infrastructure and Security Reverse Trade Mission (RTM) will bring up to twelve delegates from the Indonesian civil aviation sector to the United States to gain exposure to advanced air traffic management and airport infrastructure and security technology, services and best practices through visits to U.S. facilities and meetings with leading U.S. companies. The purpose of this RTM was to bring procurement officials to the United States to witness U.S. technology and ingenuity firsthand and develop the relationships with U.S. companies necessary to spur increased exports.

title: Transport Services
long_description: Support and strengthen reliable and affordable transport systems.

title: Technical Assistance - Aviation Safety, through Aviation Safety Oversight Group, Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AVIATION SAFETY, THROUGH AVIATION SAFETY OVERSIGHT GROUP, INC.
long_description: This $565,534 Aviation Safety Technical Assistance (TA) grant is assisting the Civil Aviation Authority of Vietnam (CAAV) in enhancing its aviation safety oversight capabilities in order to help Vietnam meet standards established by the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO). The Aviation Safety Oversight Group, Inc. (AVSOG) is reviewing critical elements of aviation safety oversight and conducting extensive formal classroom and on-the-job training, consistent with an inspector indoctrination course, for operations, airworthiness, and licensing inspectors. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Infrastructure and Transport Services - Transport Services
short_description: INFRASTRUCTURE AND TRANSPORT SERVICES - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: Sararogah-Janjal-makin road, Ghulam Khan-Bannu road,Wana-Makin road, Repair of flood damaged tank, and Reconstruction of Gomal bridge

title: Feasibility Study - Gagan Extension Business Case, through KPMG Consulting, LLC
short_description: FEASIBILITY STUDY - GAGAN EXTENSION BUSINESS CASE, THROUGH KPMG CONSULTING, LLC
long_description: This feasibility study grant funds the GAGAN Extension Business Case project for the Airports Authority of India (AAI). AAI is seeking to establish a business case for the extension of its Global Positioning System (GPS) Aided Geo-Augmented Navigation (GAGAN) system, which is used primarily by the aviation sector in India. AAI has a medium- to long-term objective to realize full utilization of GAGAN in India's aviation sector and to offer similar services to prospective users within India in other sectors as well as aircraft overflying neighboring countries in South Asia and Southeast Asia. This study aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Interest Payments: USAID - Country Loans
long_description: Interest Payments: USAID - Country Loans [Republic of the Philippines]

title: Technical Assistance - APEC Air Traffic Management Emissions Reduction, through Metron Aviation, Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - APEC AIR TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT EMISSIONS REDUCTION, THROUGH METRON AVIATION, INC.
long_description: The Air Traffic Management Emissions Reduction Technical Assistance (TA) project applied Air Traffic Flow Management (ATFM) and Collaborative Decision Making (CDM) tools to analyze aviation traffic flow patterns for the Philippines and Vietnam and recommended ATFM and CDM solutions to reduce aviation emissions on major air routes within and between these two countries. By reducing en route times, airborne holding, and airport queuing delays, ATFM and CDM can reduce emissions in congested airspace. This TA aimed to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Technical Assistance - Provision 2 Body Scanner System Pilot Project, through L-3 Communications Security & Detention Systems Inc.
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - PROVISION 2 BODY SCANNER SYSTEM PILOT PROJECT, THROUGH L-3 COMMUNICATIONS SECURITY & DETENTION SYSTEMS INC.
long_description: This $712,456 grant is supporting a technical assistance (TA) for the Airports Authority of India and L-3 Communications -Security and Detection Systems (L-3) to install a ProVision 2 body scanner at Delhi International Airport. The project demonstrates ProVision 2's ability to effectively detect potential metallic and non-metallic object threats within the context of India's airport environment. The operational deployment of the technology will also include training for airport security staff on the use and adjustment of the advanced equipment to accommodate India's operating passenger environment. This TA aims to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Principal Repayments: USAID - Country Loans
long_description: Principal Repayments: USAID - Country Loans [Republic of the Philippines]

title: Reverse Trade Mission - Freight Rail Operations, through Koeppen, Elliot & Associates, Ltd.
short_description: REVERSE TRADE MISSION - FREIGHT RAIL OPERATIONS, THROUGH KOEPPEN, ELLIOT & ASSOCIATES, LTD.
long_description: This funding supports two rail sector reverse trade missions (RTM) for delegates from China's National Railways Administration (NRA) and various state-owned railway development companies in China. The RTMs were intended to familiarize delegates with U.S. freight rail system practices, equipment, and technology.

title: Philippine-American Fund Grant Facility - Transport Services
short_description: PHILIPPINE-AMERICAN FUND GRANT FACILITY - TRANSPORT SERVICES
long_description: The five-year Philippine-American Fund Grant Facility promotes sustainable development by competitively awarding grants to local organizations to sponsor activities in five broad categories - economic growth, governance, counter-trafficking, education and biodiversity conservation. The economic growth grants fund enterprise development and jobs generation. The governance grants strengthen the work of civil society organizations in promoting public sector accountability and transparency. The counter-trafficking grants assist local partners in improving trafficking prevention and prosecution and victim protection. The education grants finance scalable and technology-based solutions for quality education in hard-to-reach areas. The biodiversity grants foster conservation and management of natural resources to improve environmental resilience.

title: Technical Assistance - Aviation Technical Assistance (Ata) Workshop Series, through U.S. Chamber of Commerce
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AVIATION TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE (ATA) WORKSHOP SERIES, THROUGH U.S. CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
long_description: This $240,000 technical assistance provided to key officials from the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC), National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC), airport management groups, and individual airports in China consisted of eight aviation-related workshops. The workshops provided technical assistance in a broad range of managerial, technical, safety, efficiency, capacity and operational training. The workshops were intended to serve as a forum where representatives from U.S. industry and government could engage Chinese counterparts to collaborate on best practices, and standards and policy development for the aviation industry. This technical assistance aimed to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

title: Peshawar-Torkham Road Additional Work - Transport Services
short_description: PESHAWAR-TORKHAM ROAD ADDITIONAL WORK - TRANSPORT SERVICES

title: Technical Assistance - Air Simulation For The New Istanbul Airport, through Ascendant Program Services, LLC
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - AIR SIMULATION FOR THE NEW ISTANBUL AIRPORT, THROUGH ASCENDANT PROGRAM SERVICES, LLC
long_description: This desk study finalized the Terms of Reference and budget for an Air Simulation Technical Assistance (TA) to the New Istanbul Airport. The General Directorate of Turkish Aviation (Devlet Hava Meydanlari ??letmesi) (DHM?) and ?GA Havalimani I?letmesi A.?, (IGA) the concessionaire that will operate the airport for 25-years, sought technical assistance to calibrate an air simulation tool to design optimal airspace and runway approaches to the New Istanbul Airport. The goal of the TA is to aid with drafting the operating procedures necessary to ensure that the airspace operates at sufficient capacity to meet the anticipated traffic demand in 2018.

title: Technical Symposium - 2015 U.S.-India Aviation Partnership Summit, through American Association of Airport Executives
short_description: TECHNICAL SYMPOSIUM - 2015 U.S.-INDIA AVIATION PARTNERSHIP SUMMIT, THROUGH AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF AIRPORT EXECUTIVES
long_description: The fifth U.S.-India Aviation Partnership Summit featured a two-day Summit in Bangalore, India from November 3-5, 2015. With leadership provided by the U.S.-India Aviation Cooperation Program (ACP), the Summit received high-level support and attendees from India's civil aviation agencies (Ministry of Civil Aviation, Directorate General of Civil Aviation, Airports Authority of India, and Bureau of Civil Aviation Security), key industry members in the aviation sector and government. The purpose of this summit was to introduce procurement officials to U.S. technology and ingenuity firsthand and develop the relationships with U.S. companies necessary to spur increased exports.

title: Definitional Mission - Transportation Infrastructure, through Emerging Markets Infrastructure, LLC
short_description: DEFINITIONAL MISSION - TRANSPORTATION INFRASTRUCTURE, THROUGH EMERGING MARKETS INFRASTRUCTURE, LLC
long_description: This definitional mission (DM) identified and evaluated opportunities in the transportation sector in Indonesia for USTDA funding consideration. The DM contractor assessed prospective projects in transportation infrastructure development that would advance economic development as well as U.S. commercial interests in Indonesia.

title: Technical Assistance - Security Vulnerability Assessments for Airports, through New Age Security Solutions
short_description: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE - SECURITY VULNERABILITY ASSESSMENTS FOR AIRPORTS, THROUGH NEW AGE SECURITY SOLUTIONS
long_description: This $529,850 technical assistance (TA) grant supported the Department of Transport and Communications (DOTC) in conducting security vulnerability assessments at three representative airports in the Philippines. The TA recommended technical/performance specifications for equipment and implementation plans to modernize and upgrade airport security procedures. This project is expected to serve as a model for broader application throughout the country for more secure and efficient airport operations. This TA aimed to support the development of sustainable infrastructure while also opening up the market to U.S. businesses that could supply state-of-the-art goods and services for project implementation.

Ltd., a private sector port and freight logistics operator, with an assessment of the infrastructure, operational, and business requirements of acquiring and operating a fleet of locomotives and wagons on the existing Karachi-Lahore Railway. The Construction of Flyovers (Additional Loan)
long_description: The project consists of the civil works for flyovers and slopes, including: a. Constructing a flyover at the Moqabazar intersection, 2,225 m in length, consisting of four lanes and two sidewalks. b. Constructing a flyover at the Motchak intersection, consisting of two levels and two sidewalks: • The first level is 3,157 m long and has four lanes. • The second level is 430 m long and has two lanes. c. Constructing a traffic circle consisting of four lanes and two sidewalks, with a length of 1858 m. d. Constructing 15 slopes on different levels and a length of 1000 m.

title: Chella Bandi - Patika Road
long_description: The project also includes the construction of a road with a length of 109.2 km and a width of 5.5-6.1 meters, with a shoulder of a width of one meter on each side. It includes the construction of another two lane road with a length of 88.8 km from Muzaffarabad to Athmuqam. The project also includes construction of 2 tunnels with a length of 4.3 km and a bridge with a length of 0.15 km and an entrance to the two tunnels with a length of 200 m, in addition to provision of design related advisory services and supervision of the project execution.

title: Banglasesh Sasec Road Connectivity Project

The OPEC fund and Asian Bank are funding the first 3 packages (60 km) from Joydevpur-Chandra-Tangail.
